flashcards.definition: Coal, oil, and natural gas; nonrenewable energy sources formed from ancient organic matter over millions of years.
Solid fossil fuel; dirtiest per unit energy; used mainly for electricity generation; produces more CO₂, SO₂, and particulates.
Cleanest-burning fossil fuel (~50% less CO₂ than coal); primarily methane (CH₄); growing share of energy.
Injecting high-pressure fluid into rock to fracture it and release oil/gas; concerns about water contamination and earthquakes.
Precipitation with pH < 5.6 caused by SO₂ and NOₓ from fossil fuel combustion reacting with atmospheric water.
